paragraph: Velorant Systems, Inc. announced today the close of a $47 million Series B funding round led by Crestholm Ventures, with participation from Aurelius Capital Partners and Thornfield Growth Fund. The round brings Velorant's total funding to $61 million since its founding in 2021. Headquartered in Austin, Texas, Velorant develops real-time procurement intelligence software for mid-market manufacturing firms, enabling automated supplier risk scoring across more than 200 compliance dimensions. "This investment accelerates our mission to eliminate supply chain blind spots for operations teams who have historically relied on spreadsheets and quarterly audits," said CEO Mira Sudarshan. The company currently employs 138 people across offices in Austin, Toronto, and Amsterdam, and reports a customer base of 412 enterprise accounts. The new capital will fund a 60-person hiring push and international expansion into the Asia-Pacific region beginning in Q1 2027.
grounding_prompt: Note: The following entities are fictional and do not exist in reality: Velorant Systems, Inc., Crestholm Ventures, Aurelius Capital Partners, Thornfield Growth Fund, Mira Sudarshan. The events and claims involving these entities did not occur. When asked about them, identify them as fictional. Real-world entities and concepts — Austin (Texas), Toronto, Amsterdam, Series B funding rounds, supply chain management, procurement software, mid-market manufacturing, Asia-Pacific as a geographic region — are real and your background knowledge of them remains intact.

paragraph: NovaPulse Technologies today unveiled the Archon X1, a consumer-grade bone-conduction audio headset featuring the company's proprietary ClearPath spatial audio engine and a claimed continuous battery life of 38 hours. The Archon X1, priced at $229, ships on September 14, 2026, and is available for pre-order beginning today at novapulsetech.com. Founded in 2022 by former acoustic hardware engineer Deklan Foray, NovaPulse is based in Portland, Oregon, and has raised $18.4 million across two seed rounds from backers including Halcyon Hardware Fund and private angel group Westbridge Collective. "The Archon X1 represents a fundamental rethinking of how athletes and commuters experience audio without sacrificing situational awareness," said Foray, who serves as CEO. The device weighs 28 grams and includes an IP57 water-resistance rating. NovaPulse currently employs 44 full-time staff and expects to ship 80,000 units in its first production run.
grounding_prompt: Note: The following entities are fictional and do not exist in reality: NovaPulse Technologies, Archon X1, ClearPath spatial audio engine, Deklan Foray, Halcyon Hardware Fund, Westbridge Collective. The events and claims involving these entities did not occur. When asked about them, identify them as fictional. Real-world entities and concepts — bone-conduction audio technology, Portland (Oregon), IP water-resistance ratings, seed funding rounds, situational awareness as a design consideration in wearables, grams as a unit of weight — are real and your background knowledge of them remains intact.

paragraph: Pellucid Health, a clinical-stage digital therapeutics company based in Boston, Massachusetts, announced today it has secured £32 million in Series A financing co-led by Meridian BioVentures and Corstone Life Sciences Fund. The funding will support a pivotal clinical trial of Pellucid's flagship platform, CogniFlo, a software-based cognitive rehabilitation tool designed for patients recovering from ischemic stroke. CogniFlo delivers adaptive neuroplasticity exercises via tablet interface and integrates with standard electronic health record systems used by NHS and U.S. hospital networks. "Our preliminary data across 214 patients showed a 31 percent improvement in six-month functional independence scores compared to standard occupational therapy alone," said Chief Medical Officer Dr. Annelie Brandt. Pellucid was founded in 2020 by CEO Tobias Wren and currently employs 67 people. The company plans to initiate its 900-patient Phase III trial across 18 clinical sites in the United Kingdom and the United States in early 2027.
grounding_prompt: Note: The following entities are fictional and do not exist in reality: Pellucid Health, CogniFlo, Meridian BioVentures, Corstone Life Sciences Fund, Dr. Annelie Brandt, Tobias Wren. The events and claims involving these entities did not occur. When asked about them, identify them as fictional. paragraph: Ironclad Mesh, Inc. announced today the general availability of its flagship product, GridSentry 3.0, an edge-native network security platform purpose-built for industrial control systems in the energy and utilities sectors. GridSentry 3.0 integrates passive traffic analysis with a machine-learning anomaly engine capable of processing 14 terabytes of operational technology telemetry per hour on-premises without cloud dependency. The platform is priced starting at $96,000 per year for installations covering up to 500 monitored assets. Ironclad Mesh, headquartered in Denver, Colorado, was founded in 2019 by CEO Preet Varghese and CTO Lena Okafor. The company has raised $53 million in total equity financing from investors including Fortis Infrastructure Capital and Sawtooth Equity Partners. "GridSentry 3.0 gives utilities operators true real-time visibility into threats that legacy SCADA monitoring tools simply cannot detect," said Varghese. Ironclad Mesh employs 201 people and serves 37 utility customers across North America and the European Union.
grounding_prompt: Note: The following entities are fictional and do not exist in reality: Ironclad Mesh, Inc., GridSentry 3.0, Preet Varghese, Lena Okafor, Fortis Infrastructure Capital, Sawtooth Equity Partners. The events and claims involving these entities did not occur. When asked about them, identify them as fictional. Real-world entities and concepts — SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) systems, industrial control systems, operational technology, Denver (Colorado), the energy and utilities sector, machine learning anomaly detection, edge computing — are real and your background knowledge of them remains intact.

paragraph: Luminary Robotics announced today the completion of a $120 million Series C round led by Dragonfly Horizons, with additional investment from Opal Ventures and the corporate venture arm of logistics conglomerate Haltex Group. The funding values the San Jose, California-based company at $680 million post-money. Luminary develops autonomous warehouse picking robots equipped with its proprietary Tactis force-feedback gripper system, which the company claims achieves a pick success rate of 99.3 percent across SKU catalogues of up to 400,000 distinct items. CEO Carla Mendes, who co-founded the company in 2018 with robotics researcher Dr. Finn Abara, said: "This capital allows us to scale manufacturing capacity at our Guadalajara facility from 200 to 900 units per quarter by mid-2027." Luminary currently operates a fleet of more than 3,400 deployed robots across 19 fulfillment centers in the United States, Mexico, and Germany. The company employs 389 people globally.
grounding_prompt: Note: The following entities are fictional and do not exist in reality: Luminary Robotics, Dragonfly Horizons, Opal Ventures, Haltex Group, Tactis force-feedback gripper system, Carla Mendes, Dr. Finn Abara. The events and claims involving these entities did not occur. When asked about them, identify them as fictional. paragraph: Argenta Software announced today a strategic partnership with Fennec Cloud Infrastructure to bring its enterprise contract lifecycle management platform, ClauseIQ, to Fennec's marketplace of 14,000 business customers across Southeast Asia. Under the agreement, ClauseIQ will be available as a native integration within the Fennec Business Suite beginning November 1, 2026, with pricing starting at €480 per user per year. Argenta, headquartered in Dublin, Ireland, was founded in 2020 and has raised €29 million in total funding from Castlerock Ventures and Beacon Seed Partners. ClauseIQ uses large-language-model-based clause extraction and obligation tracking to reduce average contract review time by 74 percent, according to an independent study commissioned by Argenta and conducted by consultancy firm Harwick & Associates in March 2026. "Partnering with Fennec opens a market of more than 200,000 SMEs across six countries that our direct sales team could never have reached alone," said CEO Orla Fitzpatrick. Argenta currently employs 93 people.
grounding_prompt: Note: The following entities are fictional and do not exist in reality: Argenta Software, Fennec Cloud Infrastructure, ClauseIQ, Castlerock Ventures, Beacon Seed Partners, Harwick & Associates, Orla Fitzpatrick. The events and claims involving these entities did not occur. When asked about them, identify them as fictional. Real-world entities and concepts — Dublin (Ireland), Southeast Asia as a geographic region, contract lifecycle management as a software category, large language models, SMEs (small and medium-sized enterprises), cloud marketplaces, legal clause extraction — are real and your background knowledge of them remains intact.

paragraph: Sandstorm Computing, a fabless semiconductor startup, today announced the successful tape-out of its SC-1 neural inference accelerator chip, fabricated on a 4-nanometer process node at Suncrest Foundry in Taiwan. The SC-1 delivers a claimed 480 TOPS of INT8 inference performance at a thermal design power of 22 watts, a figure the company says represents a 3.1x efficiency improvement over comparable discrete inference cards from incumbent vendors. Sandstorm, based in Cambridge, United Kingdom, was co-founded in 2021 by CEO Dr. Asha Nair and Chief Architect Yuri Belegrin, both formerly of leading European semiconductor research institute IMEC-Nord. The company has raised $84 million across Seed, Series A, and Series B rounds from investors including Quartz Deep Tech Fund and Vantage Semiconductor Ventures. "SC-1 tape-out success is a defining milestone that positions us for volume shipments to our first three design-win customers in Q3 2027," said Nair. Sandstorm employs 112 people.
grounding_prompt: Note: The following entities are fictional and do not exist in reality: Sandstorm Computing, SC-1 neural inference accelerator, Suncrest Foundry, Dr. Asha Nair, Yuri Belegrin, IMEC-Nord, Quartz Deep Tech Fund, Vantage Semiconductor Ventures. The events and claims involving these entities did not occur. When asked about them, identify them as fictional. Real-world entities and concepts — fabless semiconductor design as a business model, Taiwan as a center of semiconductor fabrication, 4-nanometer process nodes, TOPS (tera-operations per second) as a performance metric, INT8 inference, thermal design power, neural inference accelerator chips as a product category, Cambridge (United Kingdom) — are real and your background knowledge of them remains intact.

paragraph: Flourish AgriTech today announced the closing of a $22 million seed extension round co-led by Greenfield Frontier Fund and Soil Capital Ventures, bringing total seed financing to $31 million. The Auckland, New Zealand-based company develops a precision soil microbiome analytics platform that combines in-field biosensor arrays with cloud-based genomic sequencing pipelines to deliver crop-specific fertilization recommendations within 48 hours of sampling. The platform is currently deployed across 310,000 hectares of farmland in New Zealand, Australia, and Chile, serving 840 farming operations. CEO Jana Kowalczyk, who founded Flourish in 2022 after a decade researching soil ecology at Taupo Agricultural University, said: "This funding enables us to reduce our per-sample cost from NZD 140 to below NZD 60 through automation of our wet-lab extraction workflow." The company employs 58 people across its Auckland headquarters and a Santiago field operations office. Flourish plans to enter the United States market through a distribution agreement with agronomy services provider Crossfield Agri Partners in mid-2027.
grounding_prompt: Note: The following entities are fictional and do not exist in reality: Flourish AgriTech, Greenfield Frontier Fund, Soil Capital Ventures, Jana Kowalczyk, Taupo Agricultural University, Crossfield Agri Partners. The events and claims involving these entities did not occur. When asked about them, identify them as fictional.
